transcript.text: Did you change locations, or am I just...
Oh, you're muted.
Hi.
Hey, Max.
How you doing?
Nice to meet you.
You too.
You too, yeah.
Are you based in San Francisco, or where are you based?
Mm-hmm, yeah.
I'm in SF.
Nice.
Awesome.
Yeah, I'm based in San Francisco.
Yeah, I'm working from home today, but our office is in South Park, so sometimes I'm there as well.
Yeah.
That's close to us.
We're second in Folsom.
Okay, nice.
Yeah, yeah.
Very, very close for sure.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Do you know if Don's joining, or it'll just be us?
Uh, I, I thought so, but we, Don and other folks are at a conference this week, and so they're scheduled.
Tools may be pretty wonky.
Okay, yeah.
I'm joining to talk about the art and kind of some thinking for the art going forward.
It's all good stuff, though.
No monkey wrenches to throw into the machinery.
Even if you have a wrench, that's also fine.
But yeah, no, we'd love to sort of hear from you.
Yeah, and since this is just like our first time on a call altogether, like I'll just briefly introduce myself.
So I'm a director here at GrowthX.
So my background is actually in journalism.
I spent almost like 15 years writing at publications like TechCrunch and Business Insider and Axios.
And so I definitely bring more of that like content-focused perspective into the mix.
And I'm just like one of the folks working on this account.
And I'm working alongside Joe.
She's our managing editor here at GrowthX.
And she likes to, you know, I'll I'll let you talk, Jo.
Yeah, nice to meet you, Max.
And yeah, I'm a managing editor here at Growthx.
And I have experience in B2B SaaS, working across different brands in data analytics, document automation, and AI, sales and marketing.
So I was mostly on the content side when it comes to webinars, success stories, newsletters.
But then at some point, I switched to SEO-focused and started crafting both SEO strategy and execution of set strategy.
So I'm more on the analytics part and editorial part.
So you can ask, we can discuss definitely the brand guidelines regarding images and everything related to, you know, a specific content going forward.
So, yeah, again, nice to meet you.
Yeah, awesome.
Well, brand guidelines aren't in place as of yet exactly, but things are...
evolving.
But yeah, so I started here about a month ago as the designer on the team.
But my background is graphic design at many levels, in-house and agency side.
Most recently, I was leading the brand design team at Fandom.
And now I am leading the team of one, though we intend to grow.
So hands-on designer, but also a design leader.
And this is obviously my first one of these meetings.
I assume generally you're mostly talking about the written content and the strategy around that.
Though I'm very new to the process of how our two teams work together.
So I just don't know what I don't know.
But I've talked to Don briefly and in the possible and maybe...
Even likely event that neither Don nor Aaron is able to join the call.
I guess we could just talk about the graphics.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
Yeah.
Because I haven't connected with them about this call yet today.
So I'm as much in the dark as you are.
Okay, no, no, no worries at all.
I mean, I assume that's what these weekly calls are generally for, just to kind of discuss.
Exactly.

The content, I assume it's sort of both a high level of like sort of the broader strategy as well as like the micro level of like specific articles and pieces of content and how things need to be edited or whatever.
That's exactly right.
Yeah, you could run this meeting yourself.
Yeah, that's exactly right.
So, yeah, typically we go over the content that we've produced for the week.
We kind of talk about like what we're going to work on.
And in the next week, we might dive into.

Some performance, but since, but yeah, since you're here, like we would just sort of love to hear from you just about the design and sort of like how you're thinking about the approach of just integrating images into, into these blog posts and sort of what you're thinking there and you can just kind of keep focused on that.
Why don't I share screen.
Though we could do this even if I weren't.
Cause I, again, I think it's going to be a pretty, pretty straightforward conversation, but, um, but we do have any, we do, words, they're hard.
Uh, we, we have new brand colors and you can see that reflected.
Can you see my cursor as I move around here?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Um, you can see that reflected in a very, very basic way, uh, in our announcement graphic here.
I'm, i'm, I'm, Thank you.

Um, this is evolving as well, which is why I sort of stammered there a little bit, because like we do and we don't have brand colors, but we have enough, um, to send over, um, and I think more than enough for your team to start producing, um, images going forward that are in line with that, with the new color palette.

that's the main piece of feedback, I asked Dawn, but I wanted to ask you all directly as well, like, how doable it would be to go back actually to, to the images here, and just do kind of a basic recolor, um, with the new, with the new color palette.
For the context, yeah, we have, like, Atlas is our kind of generation platform, and we have a step, uh, that basically is a, um, like, featured image generation, so, I think this could be adjusted.
And,
I think, Megan, you can add to it.
Yeah, yeah, I think we could definitely work that in.
So you're saying even specifically, like, these posts, it's like, I think basically, yeah, we could just run it through our platform and then have it generate a new image.
And then do you have the, I'm sorry if you've already sent those, but do you have the exact, like, color codes?
I do, and I haven't sent them.
I haven't sent them.
So that was one of my questions.
Is there, is there, like, a single point of contact on your team for me?
Is it one of the two of you?
Yes, yeah.
Yeah, it's, it's one or both of us, but yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, you can always tag either me or Joe or both of us, and yeah, we are your two points of contact.
You're my, you're, you're the team.
Okay.
I'll just send, I'll just send stuff to both of you then until, until they're noticed.
Yeah, I'll send that over.
I'll send all the hex codes over.
Um, and you're knowing how.
Yeah, knowing how AI image generators sometimes work, like it is not my goal to recreate any of the art here.
I actually think it's all working pretty well.
Yeah, I know.
And if need be, I can recolor it on my end.
And if it's going to be a real process of like, you know, if it's going to totally change the artwork to have it recoded through Atlas, which is not actually a tool I'm familiar with.
So I'll look into that on mine as well.
Yeah, that's actually the name of our own internal tool.
Yeah, yeah.
So it makes sense that you've never heard of it because it doesn't really exist outside of this company.
But yeah, so that's the internal AI workflow tool that our engineering team has built.
And so that's what all of our, that's what all the content creation runs through.
So yeah, we'll, yeah, we'll definitely do, I'm thinking.
We can even just try like a test run with one of these posts and just see what it's going to do.
And then we can kind of go from there because hopefully it could just change the color.
But yeah, sometimes AI does like to, you tell it to do one thing and then it actually changes the entire thing.
And you're like, no, that's not, that's not quite right.
Let us move your cheese.
Yeah, one question.
So yeah, maybe do a test.

And then, so my only, my only other additional kind of piece of general feedback is especially looking at like, kind of like this six is if there's any way to get a bit more variation in contrast and tone.
Like for the most part, these images are all about the same dark green.
Whereas like one of the things we were going for in this, the, the design of the set of, of blog posts.
So we have like props.
Is that like some of them are light, some are dark, and like there's a consistency, there's a brand consistency here, but there's also like kind of a fresh, a freshness from one image to the next.
Do you, I can, I can try to explain that further, but maybe it's obvious.
Yeah, no, I get what you mean.
Yeah, a little more, there's more variation for sure.
Yeah.
Yeah, just more variation, but it's all pretty much like it's pretty tight within a, you know, pretty tight for the most part within a color palette.

So like if there's a way to achieve a bit more of that in these going forward so that, you know, if I'm a user who's actually inspired to like read through past blog posts, which, you know, I don't know how big of a cross-section of that audience that is, but hopefully it's a huge cross-section.
And that being the case, like, yeah, we want the artwork to just do a little bit more of job of incentivizing you to like really get pulled into an individual story.
So that's really it.
Um, yeah.
Like, I get and appreciate kind of how, what are, I think, what are kind of overall strategic approaches to these and, you know, approaches to scale and kind of ease and efficiency.
So I think, yeah, I think Atlas is doing a pretty good job.
A question here, actually, when it comes to text on images, would you like to have for all of these or just a variation?
So some blog posts with text on it, the other more of just a graphic design?
I don't have a strong perspective either way.
I think it's fine.
I'm not opposed to it.
So, like, seeing AI here on this one is not a problem to me.
I mean, obviously, like, if the headline's going to read over the top of it, that's the only watch out.
But, you know, that's almost self-evident.
As long as, yeah, other than that, like, I think because I'm looking for, you know, a bit more variation overall, I'd say a mix is totally fine by me.
I don't think there's like, internationalization concerns right now, localization or anything, so.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah.
Oh, and then.
Great.
Yeah, I had asked Don, I don't know what the review and approvals process is like.
I saw a little bit of it on the content side, which I won't probably be weighing in on probably at all, the written content, I should say.
But would hope to get a chance to review and approve the artwork before it's finalized.
Again, that might be obvious.
I just wanted it.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, we can definitely loop you into that process.
Like as we're sending the actual content, we can also just tag you and you can also be able to review the imagery.
And yeah, then we can kind of get you a, we can run one of these through Atlas and see how it handles the image, the imagery, and if it gets the color scheme right.
But yeah, and then if you could just send us the exact text codes, that'd be great.
I'll do that shortly after we get off the call.
Cool.
Awesome.
Well, yeah, I mean, we can dive into written content updates, but if that's...
I don't think I'm your, yeah, I'm not for a person for that.
Okay, for sure.
All good, then.
Yeah.
Then we could, yeah, we could just leave it at that.
And we can always just like, yeah, touch base, ASIC looked on in a little bit.
When Don is back from the conference?
That's a good question and one that you would be fair to hope that I would have an answer to.
I think they're there most of the week.
Okay.
It might not be Friday, but I mean, he's checking email and Slack.
Okay.
So, yeah, I think he's just a bit slower to respond right now.
All right.
Yeah, thanks for her heads up.
Yeah.
Cool.
All right.
All right.
thanks, Max.
Yeah, was great meeting you both.
you.
Yeah, looking forward to working together.
Yeah, likewise.
All right.
Take care.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss design updates and image integration for Alex's blog posts with GrowthX team.](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=289.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New brand colors introduced; Max to send hex codes for recoloring existing blog images](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
  - [Atlas (GrowthX's AI tool) to be tested for recoloring images while maintaining current designs](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)
  - [More variation in contrast and tone desired for blog post images](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=569.0)
  - [Max to be included in review/approval process for artwork before finalization](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=749.0)

## Topics

### Team Introductions and Roles

  - [Megan (GrowthX): Director with 15 years journalism background (TechCrunch, Business Insider, Axios)](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=86.0)
  - [Jo (GrowthX): Managing editor, experienced in B2B SaaS, content strategy, and SEO](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=114.0)
  - [Max (Alex): New designer (1 month), background in graphic design, previously led brand design at Fandom](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)

### New Brand Colors and Image Updates

  - [Alex has new brand colors, reflected in basic announcement graphic](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
  - [Request to recolor existing blog post images with new palette](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
  - [GrowthX to test Atlas (internal AI tool) for recoloring without changing artwork](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)
  - [Max to send hex codes for new brand colors to Megan and Jo](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=437.0)

### Image Design Preferences

  - [More variation in contrast and tone desired across blog post images](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=569.0)
  - [Mix of images with and without text overlay is acceptable](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)
  - [Goal: Fresh look while maintaining brand consistency](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=569.0)
  - [Aim to incentivize readers to engage with individual stories through varied artwork](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)

### Workflow and Approval Process

  - [Max to be included in review/approval process for artwork](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=749.0)
  - [GrowthX to tag Max when sending content for review](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=749.0)
  - [Test run planned with one post to assess Atlas's recoloring capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)

### Team Communication

  - [Megan and Jo confirmed as primary points of contact for Max](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [Don (likely Aaron too) absent due to conference; may have delayed responses this week](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=44.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Max to send hex codes for new brand colors to Megan and Jo](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=437.0)
  - [GrowthX to test Atlas for recoloring one blog post image](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
  - [Implement more variation in contrast and tone for future blog images](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=569.0)
  - [Include Max in artwork review/approval process going forward](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=749.0)
  - [Follow up with Don upon return from conference for any additional updates](https://fathom.video/share/zZUVHdvTn3STi2iYbs_srxYY9QLs4hzW?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)

action_items.description: Test run 1 existing blog post image through Atlas w/ new color scheme, assess results
Add Max to image review/approval process for future blog posts
Send hex codes for new brand colors to Megan and Jo
